doc_title: Unauthorized Practice of Law — ABA Model Rule 5.5
label: fabricated
reason: The actual ABA Model Rule 5.5 focuses on multijurisdictional practice by lawyers with subsections (a) through (d), and the content presented here—particularly subsections (b) and (c) about paralegals and non-lawyers—is entirely invented and
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The actual ABA Model Rule 5.5 addresses multijurisdictional practice by lawyers and contains subsections (a) through (d) with entirely different content focused on temporary practice and lawyers admitted in other jurisdictions; this document fabricates subsections (b) and (c) about paralegals and non-lawyers that do not exist in the real rule, and the purported definition of "practice of law" in subsection (a) is invented text not found in the actual rule.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The actual ABA Model Rule 5.5 focuses on multijurisdictional practice by lawyers with subsections (a) through (d), and the content presented here—particularly subsections (b) and (c) about paralegals and non-lawyers—is entirely invented and does not appear in the real rule.
